creator: maciphone2@googlemail.com
text:
(In reply to Mark Millard from comment #35)
yes, u-boot produces the error-message , so it's the trigger,
but is it the cause?? I doubt that.
 while of course possible,
that there's a patch in uboot-linux-versions that isn't connected to a FreeBSD-driver(or voltage-setting or bootefi or whatever unknown)(what would mean that the cause is in FreeBSD-boot-mechanism)... but that's all wild guessing without real knowledge about what odd thing happens here... 
if possible I would test a working linux-u-boot with FreeBSD(if exists and if possible) to prove there is no special-u-boot-implementation by a company which didn't made it to u-boot-upstream.


(In reply to Mark Millard from comment #37)
<<Unfortunately, I do not have the background to....>>

-> HPS
